vocabulary.example: It was sweltering hot, especially in the large classroom where they did their written papers.

vocabulary.definition: to put a magic spell on something
vocabulary.example: They had been given special, new quills for the exams, which had been bewitched with an AntiCheating spell.

vocabulary.definition: a hidden door in a floor or ceiling

vocabulary.example: His whole body rigid, he swayed where he stood and then fell flat on his face, stiff as a board.

vocabulary.definition: a thin, curling stem or arm of a plant
vocabulary.example: the plant had started to twist snakelike tendrils around her ankles.

vocabulary.definition: giving up something important to achieve a greater goal
to win at chess by trapping the opponent's king
vocabulary.example: I take one step forward and she’ll take me—that leaves you free to checkmate the king, Harry!

vocabulary.definition: to stay hidden while waiting or watching
vocabulary.example: Harry nodded, but he couldn’t shake off a lurking feeling that there was something he’d forgotten to do, something important.
outside the third floor corridor—and the door was already ajar.

vocabulary.definition: to run as fast as you can over a short distance
vocabulary.example: ” said Ron, but Harry, sprinting across the grounds toward the forest, didn’t answer.

vocabulary.definition: a piece of magic put on an object or place
vocabulary.example: “I suppose you think you’re harder to get past than a pack of enchantments!

vocabulary.definition: to force a student to leave school permanently
vocabulary.example: any more nighttime wanderings and I will personally make sure you are expelled.

characters.name: Harry Potter
characters.description: Realizes Hagrid was tricked into revealing how to calm Fluffy and decides to reach the Stone first.

characters.name: Ron Weasley
characters.description: Insists on coming along and sacrifices himself in the giant wizard chess game.

characters.name: Hermione Granger
characters.description: Uses logic to solve Snape's potion puzzle and goes back for help.

characters.name: Neville Longbottom
characters.description: Tries to stop the trio from leaving and is hit with the Body Bind curse.

background.hook: Exams end in the sweltering heat, but Harry suddenly understands how a stranger tricked Hagrid. With Dumbledore gone to London, Harry, Ron, and Hermione go through the trapdoor alone — past Fluffy, Devil's Snare, flying keys, living chessmen, and a logic puzzle — to stop Snape from stealing the Stone.
background.readingFocus: Watch what Harry learns from Hagrid about the hooded stranger. Notice each guardian they face and how Ron's chess skill and Hermione's logic keep them moving forward.

options.text: a hidden door in a floor or ceiling
put a magic spell on it
giving up something important for a greater goal

comprehension.stem: What is this chapter mainly about?

options.text: After learning Hagrid was tricked, the trio goes through the trapdoor and past the Stone's guardians to try to stop the thief
The trio serves detention in the Forbidden Forest
Harry wins the final Quidditch match of the year

comprehension.explanation: Harry realizes the card-player stole Fluffy's secret from drunk Hagrid, then he leads Ron and Hermione through the guarded passage beneath the third floor.

comprehension.stem: What clue makes Harry rush to see Hagrid after exams?

options.text: A stranger conveniently had a dragon egg and asked about Fluffy after getting Hagrid drunk
Hedwig delivered a note from Dumbledore
McGonagall told him Snape left the school
Ron found a book about the Stone in the library

comprehension.explanation: Harry connects the dragon egg stranger to Hagrid mentioning Fluffy and playing music to calm him.

comprehension.stem: Why can't the trio warn Dumbledore right away?

options.text: He left for London after an urgent Ministry owl
He is refereeing a Quidditch match
McGonagall says he is on holiday in Devon
Filch locked them in the common room

comprehension.explanation: McGonagall says Dumbledore flew to London ten minutes ago on Ministry business.

comprehension.stem: How do they get past Fluffy?

options.text: Harry plays Hagrid's flute until the dog falls asleep
Hermione casts a sleeping spell on all three heads
They feed Fluffy dragon meat from Norbert
Ron sings a lullaby he learned from Hagrid

comprehension.explanation: Harry blows on the flute Hagrid gave him for Christmas; the dog slumps asleep when the music stops briefly.

options.text: He blocks the portrait hole and they have no time to explain
He tries to tell Filch their plan
Snape orders her to do it

comprehension.explanation: Neville stands in front of the hole ready to fight; Hermione uses Petrificus Totalus so they can leave.

comprehension.stem: What must Ron do to win the wizard chess game?

options.text: Let the white queen take him so Harry can checkmate the king
Capture every white pawn by himself
Tip over the board and run

comprehension.explanation: Ron says he must be taken by the queen to leave Harry free to checkmate.

comprehension.stem: Who goes through the black fire toward the final chamber?

options.text: Only Harry — there is enough potion for one swallow
Hermione goes ahead while Harry goes back
Ron drinks after waking up from chess

comprehension.explanation: The smallest bottle has only enough for one person; Harry sends Hermione back through the purple fire for help.

vocabulary.explanation: Exam week is sweltering hot in the classrooms.

vocabulary.stem: The exam quills had been ___ with an AntiCheating spell.
vocabulary.explanation: Special new quills are bewitched to stop cheating.

vocabulary.stem: If a door is 'ajar,' it is —
vocabulary.explanation: They find the third-floor door already ajar because Snape got past Fluffy.

vocabulary.stem: Which word is closest in meaning to 'rigid'?
vocabulary.explanation: Neville's whole body goes rigid after the Body Bind curse.

vocabulary.stem: Ron tells Harry and Hermione they have to make some ___ in chess.
vocabulary.explanation: Ron accepts that he must be sacrificed so Harry can win the game.

cloze.title: Through the Trapdoor
paragraphs: During (1) ___ exam weather, Harry realizes a hooded stranger tricked drunk Hagrid into revealing Fluffy's secret. Dumbledore has left, so Harry decides Snape is going through the (2) ___ tonight.
Under the Invisibility Cloak they pass Fluffy, Devil's Snare with curling (3) ___, flying keys, and living chessmen where Ron makes a (4) ___ so Harry can (5) ___ the king.
Hermione solves Snape's logic puzzle. The third-floor door already stood (6) ___; only Harry drinks and passes the black fire, still unable to shake a (7) ___ feeling that something is wrong.

gaps.explanation: It was sweltering hot during the written exam papers.
Harry says Snape is going through the trapdoor tonight.
Devil's Snare twists snakelike tendrils around their ankles.
Ron says you have got to make some sacrifices in chess.
Ron lets the queen take him so Harry can checkmate the king.
The door to Fluffy's corridor is already ajar.
Harry cannot shake off a lurking feeling that he forgot something important.
